question:
Fill in the blank with the missing number:

The ski lift carries skiers all the way from the bottom of the mountain to the very top of the mountain, and then drops them off so they can ski back down the mountain. If it takes a skier 15 minutes to ride the lift from the bottom to the top of the mountain, and then it takes 5 minutes to ski back down the mountain, what is the most number of times a person can ski down the mountain in _______ hours?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

In Henry's collection of music CDs, he has 3 more country CDs than rock CDs but twice as many rock CDs as classical CDs. If he has _______ country CDs, how many classical CDs make up his collection?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

It takes 320 rose petals to make an ounce of perfume. If each rose produces 8 petals, and there are _______ roses per bush, how many bushes will Fern have to harvest to make 20 12-ounce bottles of perfume?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

It took Alice three months to save up to buy new shoes. If she saved 10 dollars the first month and _______ more each month, how much did she have saved by the end of the third month?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A man is returning home from work and trying to decide which route to take.  His first route option includes _______ stoplights.  This route will take him 10 minutes if all three lights are green, but each light that is red will add 3 minutes to the trip.  The second route does not include any stoplights and takes 14 minutes.  If the man chooses the first route, how much longer will his trip be if all 3 stoplights are red?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

In her first term, Governor Sandoval gave twice as many commencement addresses as Governor Hawkins. Governor Sloan gave ten more commencement addresses than Governor Sandoval in the same amount of time. If Governor Sandoval gave _______ commencement addresses, how many commencement addresses did the three of them give altogether?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Ava planted 9 apple trees in her backyard. That's _______ more than Lily planted in her backyard. How many apple trees did they plant in their backyards collectively?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Angie is part of a household with shared expenses and contributes $42 a month for necessities. She has a salary of $80 per month. She also pays a share of the household taxes each month. At the end of this month, she had $_______ left over. How much did she pay in taxes this month?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Dani brings two and half dozen cupcakes for her 2nd-grade class.  There are 27 students (including Dani), 1 teacher, and 1 teacher’s aid.  If _______ students called in sick that day, how many cupcakes are left after Dani gives one to everyone in the class?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A rope has a length of _______ meters. Stefan cuts the rope into four equal parts, gives his mother half of the cut pieces, and subdivides the remaining pieces into two more equal parts. What's the length of each piece?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Carly is trying to get in shape to try out for the soccer team. She starts by running 2 miles a week. The second week, she runs twice as long plus 3 extra miles per week. The third week she runs 9/7 as much as she ran the second week. The week after that, she sprains her ankle and has to reduce her running time by _______ miles per week. How many miles did she run the week she was injured?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

One of the clubs at Georgia's school was selling carnations for a fundraising event. A carnation costs $0.50.  They also had a special where you could buy one dozen carnations for $4.00.  If Georgia sent a dozen carnations to each of her 5 teachers and bought one carnation for each of her _______ friends, how much money would she spend?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

The Giants baseball team is trying to make their league playoff. They have played 20 games and won 12 of them. To make the playoffs, they need to win 2/3 of their games over the season. If there are _______ games left, how many do they have to win to make the playoffs?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Danny helps his friends carpool to work. He has to drive 8 miles to the first friend's house, half that distance to the second friend's house, then _______ times the total distance driven so far to get to work. How many miles does Danny drive between the second friend's house and work?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Johnny is an engineer designing a bridge. The roadway deck for the top of the bridge needs 1600 tons of concrete to construct. The two bridge anchors need equal amounts of concrete, but only one has been built so far, using 700 tons of concrete. Johnny had to calculate how many tons of concrete the supporting pillars underneath the bridge would need, and he found that the total amount of concrete in the entire bridge will be _______ tons. How much concrete did Johnny find he needs for the supporting pillars?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Louise is hanging 30 of her pictures on the wall. She hangs some of them vertically, half of them horizontally, then hangs the remaining _______ pictures haphazardly. How many pictures did Louise hang vertically?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Nancy is filling an aquarium for her fish. She fills it halfway and goes to answer the door. While she's gone, her cat knocks the aquarium over and spills half the water in it. Then Nancy comes back and triples the amount of water in the aquarium. If the aquarium is 4 feet long, 6 feet wide, and _______ feet high, how many cubic feet of water are in the aquarium?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Nick's cell phone was initially empty but was then charged for 45 minutes and has reached a 25% charge. How much longer must the cell phone be charged to reach _______% charge?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Drew is reseeding his lawn with grass seed. One bag of grass seed covers 250 square feet of lawn. His lawn is 22 feet from the house to the curb and _______ feet from side to side. He bought four bags of seed. How many extra square feet could the leftover grass seed cover after Drew reseeds his lawn?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jenny decided to get a cat with her girlfriend.  They agreed to split all the costs down the middle except for they would each buy their own toys for the cat.  The adoption fee was $50, the vet visits cost $500 for the first year and the monthly cost of food was $25.  She bought $_______ in toys.  How much did Jenny spend on the cat in the first year?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Emily bought 9 packs of candy necklaces to give her classmates at school for Valentine’s Day. Each pack had 8 candy necklaces in it. Emily opened one pack at a time. After her classmates took as many as they wanted, there were _______ candy necklaces left. How many packs did Emily open for her classmates?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Tammy just got her hair cut. For every 14 haircuts, she gets a free additional haircut. She has gotten _______ free haircuts already. She is 5 haircuts away from another free one. How many haircuts has she gotten there?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Lily had $55 in her account. She spent $_______ on a shirt. She then went to another shop and spent thrice as much as she spent on a shirt. How much money is left in Lily's account?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

If 1 ounce of Dawn liquid soap can make 200,_______ bubbles, and Dr. Bronner's liquid soap can make twice as many bubbles per ounce as Dawn liquid soap, then how many bubbles can be made from one half ounce of an equal mixture of Dawn and Dr. Bronner's liquid soaps?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A crate can hold 20 kilograms of tomatoes. Tommy has _______ crates which he bought for $330. He wants to sell the tomatoes for $6 per 1 kilogram but 3 kilograms of tomatoes were rotten. How much will be Tommy's profit from selling those tomatoes?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

James decides to try and collect rare artifacts in the field.  He spends 6 months researching before going on a 2-year-long expedition for his first find.  The second artifact takes _______ times as long for the research and discovery.  How long did it take him to find both?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Roman the Tavernmaster has $20 worth of gold coins. He sells 3 gold coins to Dorothy. After she pays him, he has $_______. How many gold coins does Roman have left?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Trip wanted to watch the new action movie in theaters.  An evening ticket cost $10 and a large popcorn & drink combo would cost him an additional $10.  He noticed on their website, they had a special offer.  From 12 noon to 3 pm, save 20% off tickets and _______% off any food combos.  How much money could Trip save by going to the earlier movie?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Charles is wondering how much chocolate milk he can make with all the supplies he finds in the fridge. He is going to keep drinking 8-ounce glasses until he uses up all the ingredients. Each glass must contain 6.5 ounces of milk and 1.5 ounces of chocolate syrup. If he has 130 ounces of milk and _______ ounces of chocolate syrup, how many total ounces ounce of chocolate milk will he drink?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Mr. Fortchaud turns on his heater on the 1st of November, 2005. The fuel tank was then full and contained 3,000 L. On January 1, _______, the tank counter indicated that 180 L remained. Mr. Fortchaud again filled his tank completely. On 1 May 2006, Mr. Fortchaud decided to stop heating and he read 1,238 L on the meter. What the volume of fuel oil that was used between 1 November 2005 and 1 May 2006?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

One dress requires 5.5 yards of fabric. Amare needs to make 4 dresses for the wedding and she has _______ feet of fabric. How many feet of fabric does Amare still need for the dresses?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jamie's father gained 5 pounds over the holidays that he wants to lose so his pants will not be as snug. He asks Jamie to help him keep track of his calories. Jamie's father burns 2,500 calories of fat a day by doing light exercise. There are 3,500 calories in a pound of body fat. How many days will it take for Jamie's father to burn off 5 pounds if he does light exercise and sticks to eating _______ calories a day?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Due to a drought, Jerry's household can only use _______000 gallons of water during July. Jerry uses 100 gallons for drinking and cooking and 20 gallons per shower. He wants to fill his pool, which measures 10 feet by 10 feet by 6 feet. If each gallon of water fills 1 cubic foot of the pool, how many showers can Jerry take in July?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Grayson has 100 questions on a test. He answers some of the questions for _______ hours, taking two minutes for each answer, then realizes he doesn't know the rest of the questions. How many questions does Grayson leave unanswered when he submits his test?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jordan's dog, Max, was born on the same day that Luka turned 4 years old.  Luka is exactly _______ years older than is Aubrey.  On Aubrey's 8th birthday, how many years old was Jordan's dog, Max?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Gina is considered a bad tipper because she tipped 5%. If good tippers tip at least 20%, how many more cents than normal would Gina have to tip on a bill of $_______ to be considered a good tipper?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Tedra is harvesting his tomatoes. He harvests 400 kg on Wednesday, and half as much on Thursday. He harvests a total of _______ kg on Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday. How many tomatoes of the number he harvested on Friday are remaining if he gives away 700kg of them to his friends?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jordan picked 5_______ mangoes from his tree. One-third of the mangoes were ripe while the other two-thirds were not yet ripe. Jordan kept 16 unripe mangoes and gave the remainder to his sister who pickled them in glass jars. If it takes 4 mangoes to fill a jar, how many jars of pickled mangoes can Jordan's sister make?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jenny's local library received 50 visitors on Monday. On Tuesday, the library received twice that number. On the remaining days of the week, an average of _______ visitors went to the library. What's the total number of visitors who went to the library that week?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Carmen needs $7 more to have twice the amount of money that Jethro has. Meanwhile, Patricia has $60, which is _______ times as much as Jethro. What is the sum of all their money?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Harry decided to buy some balloons for his friend's birthday party. One balloon costs $0.5, and a pack of 10 balloons is cheaper and costs only $3. Finally, Harry wants to bring to the party exactly _______ balloons. How much did he need to pay for them?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jerry has a bunch of half-empty soda cans on his desk. He needs to drain all of them and put them in the recycling bin. He can carry four cans at once, and it takes him 30 seconds to drain those 4 cans. It takes him ten seconds each way to walk to the sink and recycling bin and then back to his desk. If there are _______ cans on Jerry's desk, how long does it take him to throw all of them away?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A rope has a length of _______ meters. Stefan cuts the rope into four equal parts, gives his mother half of the cut pieces, and subdivides the remaining pieces into two more equal parts. What's the length of each piece?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Ed has 2 dogs, _______ cats and twice as many fish as cats and dogs combined. How many pets does Ed have in total?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Grant has worked as a freelance math worker for the last three months.  The first month he made 3_______$.  The second month he made 50$ more than double he made the first month.  The third month he quadrupled the sum of the first two months.  How much did Grant make in his first three months total?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A certain store sells computer accessories and equipment. Due to a fire outbreak in one of the factories, the price of RAM increased by 30%. After two years, the price stabilized and finally fell by 20% from what it has risen. What is the current price of RAM if it was $_______ before the fire?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Mrs. Choi purchased a house for $80000. Five years later, she sold it for a 20% profit and got a _______% broker's commission from the original price. How much did the house sell for?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

While practising for his upcoming math exams, Hayes realised that the area of a circle he had just solved was equal to the perimeter of a square he had solved in the previous problem. If the area of the circle was _______, what's the length of one side of the square?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Roman the Tavernmaster has $20 worth of gold coins. He sells 3 gold coins to Dorothy. After she pays him, he has $_______. How many gold coins does Roman have left?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Amanda's car takes five fewer minutes to complete the same trip to the beach than the bus. If the bus takes 40 minutes to drive _______ miles to the beach, how many minutes will it take the car to make a round trip?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

An Italian restaurant earns $_______ every weekday and twice as much on the weekend. How much money does it earn by the end of the month?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jonathan eats 2500 calories every day except for Saturday, when he consumes an extra 1000 calories.  He burns _______ calories every day.  What is his weekly caloric deficit?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Winston had 10 gallons of gas in his car’s gas tank. He drives to the store and uses 6 gallons of gas. Then he drives to the doctor’s office and uses 2 gallons of gas. If the tank can hold up to _______ gallons of gas, how many gallons of gas will Winston need to refill the entire tank?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

OpenAI runs a robotics competition that limits the weight of each robot. Each robot can be no more than twice the minimum weight and no less than 5 pounds heavier than the standard robot. The standard robot weighs _______ pounds. What is the maximum weight of a robot in the competition?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Marta was about to start the school year and needed to buy the necessary textbooks. She managed to buy five on sale, for $10 each. She had to order two textbooks online, which cost her a total of $_______, and three she bought directly from the bookstore for a total of three times the cost of the online ordered books. How much in total did Martha spend on textbooks?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

It takes Polly 20 minutes to cook breakfast every day. She spends 5 minutes cooking lunch. She spends 10 minutes cooking dinner 4 days this week. The rest of the days she spends _______ minutes cooking dinner. How many minutes does Polly spend cooking this week?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

John is twice as old as Mary and half as old as Tonya. If Tanya is _______, what is their average age?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Dianne runs a store that sells books.  37% of her 1000 customers end up returning their books.  Her books all cost _______ dollars apiece.  How much money does she keep in sales after subtracting the returns?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

This weekend's football game matched the Seattle Seahawks with the Denver Broncos. The final score was _______7-23, with the Seattle Seahawks taking the win. If a touchdown is worth 7 points and a field goal is worth 3 points, how many touchdowns did the Seattle Seahawks score if they scored 3 field goals during the game?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A group of 30 tourists sets out on a walking tour of the rainforest. Two tourists are eaten by anacondas, then half the remaining tourists try to pet poisonous dart frogs and get poisoned. If only 1/_______ of the poisoned tourists recovered, how many tourists are left at the end of the tour?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

During a unit on probability, a teacher surveys her class asking her students two questions.  The first question was whether they prefer dogs or cats, and the second question was whether they movies or video games.  Of the 30 students in her class, 50% chose dogs and video games as their two answers, while _______% chose dogs and movies as their two answers.  In total, how many students in this teacher's class prefer dogs over cats?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Henry needs to declutter his book collection of 99 books.  From his bookshelf, he was able to fill 3 boxes of 15 books each to take to a donation center.  He had 21 books in a room he could donate, 4 on his coffee table and 18 cookbooks stashed in the kitchen.  When he dropped them off, he noticed a box of books that had a  "free to a good home" note.  He grabbed _______ books to take back to his place.  How many books does Henry now have?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Penny's canoe can carry 6 people, but if she wants to take her dog, she will only fit 2/3 of that number inside. If every person in a trip where Penny had her dog inside the canoe weighed 1_______0 pounds, and the dog 1/4 as much weight, calculate the total weight the canoe was carrying?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Lori owns a carsharing company. There are three red cars and two white cars available to rent. Renting the white car costs $2 for every minute and the red car $_______ for every minute. All cars were rented for 3 hours. How much money did Lori earn?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Janet buys 45-pound containers of cat litter for $21 each.  If her cat litter box holds 15 pounds of cat litter, and she changes out the litter weekly, how much will it cost, in dollars, for her to buy enough litter to last _______ days?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Ahmed is 11 years old and Fouad is _______ years old. In how many years will Fouad's age be double Ahmed's current age?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Sally Draper gave her dad Don Draper 10oz of rum on his pancakes. Don can consume a maximum of _______ times that amount of rum for a healthy diet. If he already had 12oz of rum earlier that day, how many oz of rum can Don have after eating all of the rum and pancakes?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

James injured his ankle and decides to slowly start working back up to his previous running goals and then surpass them.  Before the injury, he was able to run 100 miles per week.  He wants to get up to 20% more than that total in _______ days and each week he will increase miles walked in the week by the same amount.  How many miles does he need to add per week?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Betty bought _______ kg of oranges and 3 kg of apples. The total cost of oranges was $12. The apples were two times cheaper than the oranges. How much did Betty pay for 1 kg of apples?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Trevor is currently 11 years old and his older brother is _______ years old. How old will Trevor be when his older brother is three times as old as Trevor is now?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Marco owns an ice cream truck.  His ice cream cones are $5 each.  If his expenses are 80% of total sales for the day, how many ice cream cones would he need to sell to make a $_______ profit for the day?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A rope has a length of _______ meters. Stefan cuts the rope into four equal parts, gives his mother half of the cut pieces, and subdivides the remaining pieces into two more equal parts. What's the length of each piece?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jaco wants to buy Christmas gifts for his 8 friends that are worth $9 each.  Also, he wants to buy gifts for his mother and father that are of the same amount. He has a $_______ budget to buy all gifts. How much is Jaco's budget for each of his mother and father's gift?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

There are one-third as many Ford trucks as Dodge trucks in the parking lot of the Taco Castle.  But there are twice as many Ford trucks as Toyota trucks in this parking lot, and there are half as many Volkswagen Bugs as there are Toyota trucks in this same parking lot.  If there are _______ Volkswagon Bugs in the parking lot, how many Dodge trucks are in the parking lot of the Taco Castle?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Milly is making feather boas for her dance team. Each flamingo has 20 tail feathers, and it's only safe to pluck 25% of their tail feathers at one time. If Milly needs to make 12 boas, and each boa has _______ feathers, how many flamingoes does she need to harvest?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Betty bought _______ kg of oranges and 3 kg of apples. The total cost of oranges was $12. The apples were two times cheaper than the oranges. How much did Betty pay for 1 kg of apples?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jeff makes _______ donuts each day for 12 days. Jeff eats one of the donuts each day. Chris then comes over and eats 8 donuts. If 10 donuts fit in each box, how many boxes can Jeff fill with his donuts?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Coral reads _______0 pages of a book on night 1, and 2 less than twice that on night 2.  Night 3 Coral reads 3 more pages than the sum of the first two nights.  How many total pages did Coral read in the 3 nights?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

In a parking lot, there are cars and motorcycles.  Each car has 5 wheels (including one spare) and each motorcycle has 2 wheels. There are 19 cars in the parking lot. Altogether all vehicles have _______ wheels. How many motorcycles are at the parking lot?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Tommy's home is worth 25% more than he bought it for. He sells it and buys a new house that costs $500,000. If he had to take a loan for the _______% he can't pay, how much did he buy his first house for?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

The space station, Lupus-1, is an enormous spacecraft made up of three identical cylindrical structures that house the living quarters for the crew.   The three cylindrical structures are linked together by a series of tunnels that hold them together and allow the crew to move between cylinders.  Each cylindrical structure contains 12 bedrooms, 7 bathrooms, and several kitchens.  If the entire space station has _______ rooms, how many kitchens are there on the entire space station?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jenny wants to sell some girl scout cookies and has the choice of two neighborhoods to visit.  Neighborhood A has 10 homes which each will buy _______ boxes of cookies.  Neighborhood B has 5 homes, each of which will buy 5 boxes of cookies.  Assuming each box of cookies costs $2, how much will Jenny make at the better choice of the two neighborhoods?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Bobby can deadlift 300 pounds at 13.  When he is 18 he can deadlift 100 pounds more than _______% of his previous deadlift.  How many pounds did he add per year?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jackson wants to improve his endurance running. His goal is to start by running _______ miles a day the first week, then spend the next four weeks running one additional mile/day each week. How many miles is Jackson running each day at the end of this exercise program?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

The Martin family goes to the mall to get ice cream.  A kiddie scoop is $3.  A regular scoop is $4.  A double scoop is $_______.   Mr. and Mrs. Martin each get the regular scoop.  Their two children each get the kiddie scoop.  Their three teenage children each get double scoops.  How much money does Mrs. Martin pay the cashier?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Grant spends $200.00 a year to have the newspaper delivered daily to his house.  Juanita buys the newspaper daily.  Monday through Saturday, she spends $0.50 and on Sunday she spends $_______.  How much more money does Juanita spend buying the newspaper yearly than Grant?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

At Mario's barbershop haircuts are 50% more expensive during the weekends. If Mario paid $_______ for his last haircut on Monday, how much he would have paid the day before?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Jeff has a shelter where he currently takes care of _______0 cats. On Monday he found 2 kittens in a box and took them to the shelter. On Tuesday he found 1 more cat with a leg injury. On Wednesday 3 people adopted 2 cats each. How many cats does Jeff currently have in his shelter?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

A group of six friends planned to buy a car. The cost of the car is $1700 and they plan to share the cost equally. They had a car wash to help raise funds, which would be taken out of the total cost. The remaining cost would be split between the six friends. At the car wash, they earn $_______. However, Brad decided not to join in the purchase of the car. How much more does each friend have to pay now that Brad isn't participating?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Ray has 175 cents in nickels. Ray gives _______ cents to Peter, and he gives twice as many cents to Randi as he gave to Peter. How many more nickels does Randi have than Peter?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Austin is a surfer. He took a trip to the beach during surfing season and the highest wave he caught was two feet higher than four times his height. The shortest wave he caught was four feet higher than his height. The shortest wave was three feet higher than his _______-foot surfboard is long. How tall was the highest wave Austin caught?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

At Mario's barbershop haircuts are 50% more expensive during the weekends. If Mario paid $_______ for his last haircut on Monday, how much he would have paid the day before?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Flies are Betty's frog's favorite food. Every day the frog eats 2 flies. Betty puts the flies she finds in a bottle. In the morning Betty catches 5 flies inside a bottle, and in the afternoon she catches _______ more, but when she removes the lid, one escapes.  Betty wants to gather the whole week's food for her frog. How many more flies does she need?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

John is twice as old as Mary and half as old as Tonya. If Tanya is _______, what is their average age?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Tom's rabbit can run at 25 miles per hour.  His cat can run 20 miles per hour.  The cat gets a _______-minute head start.  In hours, how long will it take for the rabbit to catch up?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Pete walks backwards three times faster than Susan walks forwards, and Tracy does one-handed cartwheels twice as fast as Susan walks forwards.  But Pete can walk on his hands only one quarter the speed that Tracy can do cartwheels.  If Pete walks on his hands at _______ miles per hour, how fast can Pete walk backwards, in miles per hour?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Andy is running late. School starts at 8:00 AM and it normally takes him 30 minutes to get there, but today he had to stop for 3 minutes each at 4 red lights and wait 10 minutes to get past construction. If he left his house at 7:_______, how many minutes late will he be?

Fill in the blank with the missing number:

Tony has $87. He needs to buy some cheese, which costs $7 a pound and a pound of beef that costs $5 a pound. After buying the beef and his cheese, he has $_______ left. How many pounds of cheese did he buy?
